body:
## Summary
- Normalize GitHub bot login variants when identifying prior AI review synthesis comments.
- Allows comments authored as `sepo-agent-app` to match an authenticated viewer reported as `sepo-agent-app[bot]`.
- Add a regression test for GitHub App bot login normalization.

## Verification
- npm --prefix .agent ci
- npm --prefix .agent test

comments.body:
I have the live PR metadata and diff; next I’m mapping only relevant rubrics to concrete evidence in the two touched files and producing the rubric scorecard.## Rubrics Review

| Total Score | Verdict | Rubrics Scored |
| -- | -- | -- |
| 100 | PASS | 5 |

| Dimension | Rubric | Result | Score | Evidence |
| -- | -- | -- | -- | -- |
| coding_workflow / generic | Make surgical changes | pass | 8/8 | PR only changes two targeted files: `.agent/src/review-summary-minimize.ts` and `.agent/src/__tests__/review-summary-minimize.test.ts`, with minimal logic/test additions tied directly to bot-login matching. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Reuse existing code | pass | 8/8 | Change extends existing flow in `isGeneratedReviewSummary(...)` by introducing small local helpers (`normalizeActorLogin`, `isSameActorLogin`) instead of duplicating matching logic across call sites. |
| coding_style / generic | Prefer simple implementations | pass | 7/7 | Normalization is a single trim + `[bot]` suffix strip and one equality helper; no extra architecture or branching beyond the needed behavior. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Read docs and linked context first | pass | 6/6 | PR body clearly scopes the intended behavior and verification commands; no parent issue/plan references appear in PR metadata/comments, so implementation appears aligned with available context. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Keep docs in sync | pass | 7/7 | No user-facing docs appear to describe this internal login-normalization detail; regression test added to document behavior contract in code (`matches GitHub App bot login variants`). |

## Notes

- Main score drivers are strong surgical scope and a focused regression test that directly covers the reported variant (`sepo-agent-app` vs `sepo-agent-app[bot]`).
- No rubric-specific follow-up needed.

## Findings

- **INFO:** No rubric violations found in this PR based on the selected applicable rubrics.

## Final Rubric Verdict

PASS

## AI Review Synthesis

<!-- sepo-agent-review-synthesis -->

> Dual-agent review by **Claude** and **Codex**.
> Requested by @lolipopshock.

## Summary of PR/Issue
- This PR fixes a mismatch when identifying prior AI review synthesis comments authored by the Sepo GitHub App.  
- The core issue is login-format asymmetry: the authenticated viewer may appear as `sepo-agent-app[bot]` while authored comments appear as `sepo-agent-app`.  
- The change normalizes bot-login variants before actor comparison in review-summary minimization logic.  
- A focused regression test was added to verify the GitHub App bot-login variant case and ensure minimization still happens correctly.  
- No parent issue, tracking issue, or broader phased plan references were found in the PR body or current discussion.

## Review
- Overall judgment across all provided reviews: the fix is correct, surgical, and sufficiently tested for the targeted behavior.  
- One reviewer noted cosmetic INFO-level suggestions (implementation style/test completeness), but no reviewer identified a blocking or warning-level defect.

| Issue | Severity | Description |
| -- | -- | -- |
| No unresolved actionable issues identified | INFO | Reviews converge on correctness and adequate regression coverage; remaining notes are optional polish, not required branch changes. |

## Progress
- PR already contains the intended normalization change and a regression test for the reported bot-login mismatch.
- Existing PR conversation does not show unresolved blocking review feedback.
- Existing inline PR comments are empty; no duplicate line comments were posted.
- Reviewer INFO notes were acknowledged as non-blocking polish ideas.

## Recommended Next Step
`NO_AUTOMATED_ACTION`: There are no unresolved actionable findings that require additional branch changes.

## Final Verdict
`SHIP`

## Action Items
- [ ] No required branch-change work remains.

## Rubrics Update

No changes were committed to `agent/rubrics` from PR #11.

I’ve finished trust and duplication checks: PR #11 has no human review comments/reviews to learn from, only bot-authored issue comments with `author_association: NONE`, so there’s no trusted durable rubric signal to add. I’m returning `no rubric changes`.no rubric changes

I reviewed PR #11 history and context (`gh pr view`, issue comments, PR comments, reviews, diff, repo owner, and collaborator/admin lookup). The conversation contains only bot-authored comments and no trusted contributor instruction indicating a durable user/team preference beyond existing rubrics. I also checked existing active rubrics under the rubrics tree and found no warranted update.

title: Fix review summary collapse bot matching
